name: Smoked Trout
recipeInstructions:
1. Smoking woods can be varied, such as Mesquite, Apple, Alder, Hickory, or any other flavor you prefer. Soak the wood in water for at least overnight.
2. Use a covered grill (a Weber grill is recommended). If using a grill instead of a smoker, arrange charcoal in two piles on either side of the cooking area.
3. Light the charcoal with a non-petroleum lighter (electric, paraffin-based firestarters, or an old two-match method).
4. In the cavity of each trout, alternate slices of lemon and lime. Oil the fish or the grill generously to facilitate easy removal later.
5. Place the fish in the middle of the charcoal fires. Add the soaked wood on top of the charcoal, close the lid, and do not open for at least 1 hour.
6. Cooking time depends on the number and size of the fish: 1 lb trout = 1 hour, 2-3 lb trout = 1.5 to 2 hours, respectively.
7. Check after an hour to ensure the trout is flaky. If not done, serve appetizers and continue smoking for another hour.
8. Use any leftover smoked trout in a salad with a spicy dressing.
